pattern: At every array index the count of positive integers missing before it is a simple formula that never decreases, so binary search finds the answer directly instead of walking the gaps one by one.
hints: At index i, work out how many positive integers are missing from the start of the number line up through just before arr[i] — it comes out to a simple formula in arr[i] and i.
That missing-count only ever grows or stays the same as i increases, which is exactly the shape binary search needs.
Binary search for the first index whose missing-count is at least k, then the answer is that index plus k.

edge_inputs.description: a leading gap followed by consecutive values
no gap at all before the requested index
array starts at one with no leading gap
k requires extending past every array element
the very first missing number sits before the array starts

stress.description: random strictly increasing arrays with a random k
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 1000))
    arr = sorted(rng.sample(range(1, 1001), n))
    k = rng.randint(1, 1000)
    return [arr, k]
